name: superq

metadata.name: superq
metadata.summary: Simple, durable, asyncio-compatible task queue inspired by Celery and Huey.

documentation:
# SuperQ

A simple, durable, asyncio-compatible task queue for Python.

classes.name: superq.wrapped_fn.WrappedFn

documentation: Schedule this function to run later on the worker server.

documentation: Register a callback function that runs when this task succeeds.

methods.name: superq.wrapped_fn.WrappedFn.on_failure
documentation: Register a callback function that runs when this task fails and is not rescheduled.

params.name: WrappedFnReturnType | Coroutine[Any, Any, WrappedFnReturnType] | Coroutine[Any, Any, None]

documentation:
Represents a function that can be scheduled to run async on a worker server.
When a function is decorated with `@task`, it is replaced with an instance of this class.

documentation: Push a new task to the queue. The task must have an `interval`.

documentation:
Pop the next task from the queue. Workers should call this method with `set_running` to "claim" a task.
Set `reschedule=True` to automatically retry the task in case of system failure (recommended).

methods.name: superq.backends.backend_base.BaseBackend.concurrency
documentation:
Return the number of active running tasks for this function.
If `with_kwargs`, only returns tasks matching the given kwargs.

Abstract base class for a task queue backend (e.g. Redis, MongoDB).

functions.name: superq.bson._machine_bytes
documentation: Get the machine portion of an ObjectId.

classes.name: superq.bson.InvalidId
documentation: Raised when trying to create an ObjectId from invalid data.

classes.name: superq.bson.ObjectId

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new ObjectId.

An ObjectId is a 12-byte unique identifier consisting of:

> - a 4-byte value representing the seconds since the Unix epoch,
> - a 3-byte machine identifier,
> - a 2-byte process id, and
> - a 3-byte counter, starting with a random value.

By default, `ObjectId()` creates a new unique identifier. The
optional parameter `oid` can be an {class}`ObjectId`, or any 12
{class}`bytes` or, in Python 2, any 12-character {class}`str`.

For example, the 12 bytes b'foo-bar-quux' do not follow the ObjectId
specification but they are acceptable input:

```
>>> ObjectId(b'foo-bar-quux')
ObjectId('666f6f2d6261722d71757578')
```

`oid` can also be a {class}`unicode` or {class}`str` of 24 hex digits:

```
>>> ObjectId('0123456789ab0123456789ab')
ObjectId('0123456789ab0123456789ab')
>>>
>>> # A u-prefixed unicode literal:
>>> ObjectId(u'0123456789ab0123456789ab')
ObjectId('0123456789ab0123456789ab')
```

Raises {class}`~bson.errors.InvalidId` if `oid` is not 12 bytes nor
24 hex digits, or {class}`TypeError` if `oid` is not an accepted type.

```
Parameters

 (optional): a valid ObjectId.
```

```
.. mongodoc:: objectids
```

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.from_datetime
documentation:
Create a dummy ObjectId instance with a specific generation time.

This method is useful for doing range queries on a field
containing {class}`ObjectId` instances.

:::{warning}
It is not safe to insert a document containing an ObjectId
generated using this method. This method deliberately
eliminates the uniqueness guarantee that ObjectIds
generally provide. ObjectIds generated with this method
should be used exclusively in queries.
:::

`generation_time` will be converted to UTC. Naive datetime
instances will be treated as though they already contain UTC.

An example using this helper to get documents where `"_id"`
was generated before January 1, 2010 would be:

```pycon
>>> gen_time = datetime.datetime(2010, 1, 1)
>>> dummy_id = ObjectId.from_datetime(gen_time)
>>> result = collection.find({"_id": {"$lt": dummy_id}})
```

```
Parameters

:  to be used
as the generation time for the resulting ObjectId.
```

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.is_valid
documentation:
Checks if a `oid` string is valid or not.

```
Parameters

: the object id to validate
```

:::{versionadded} 2.3
:::

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.__generate
documentation: Generate a new value for this ObjectId.

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.__validate
documentation:
Validate and use the given id for this ObjectId.

Raises TypeError if id is not an instance of
({class}`basestring` ({class}`str` or {class}`bytes`
in python 3), ObjectId) and InvalidId if it is not a
valid ObjectId.

```
Parameters

: a valid ObjectId
```

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.binary
documentation: 12-byte binary representation of this ObjectId.

methods.name: superq.bson.ObjectId.generation_time
documentation:
A {class}`datetime.datetime` instance representing the time of
generation for this {class}`ObjectId`.

The {class}`datetime.datetime` is timezone aware, and
represents the generation time in UTC. It is precise to the
second.

needed explicitly because __slots__() defined.

functions.name: superq.cli.main
documentation:
CLI entrypoint for the worker process. Only argument is a dot-separated path to your TaskQueue module.
Usage: superq path.to.taskqueue.module

classes.name: superq.executors.executor_asyncio.AsyncioTaskExecutor

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_asyncio.AsyncioTaskExecutor.run
documentation: Run tasks in this child process continuously until shutdown.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_asyncio.AsyncioTaskExecutor._run_aio
documentation:
Process all tasks assigned to this event loop until both queues are empty.
The "running queue" only stores the value `True` for each task that is currently running.
Wraps a child process that runs an asyncio event loop.

Add a task that runs in the event loop with the most capacity.
It is the responsibility of the caller to ensure that `capacity` is greater than 0.

Decorator to convert a function into an async task that runs on a remote worker server.
This block is executed once per function, the first time the decorated function is imported.

classes.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTaskExecutor

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTaskExecutor.submit_task
documentation: Submit a new task for execution. The caller is responsible for first checking `capacity`.

documentation: Called from inside the child process to fail all expired tasks.

documentation: Setup signal handlers in a child process.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTaskExecutor.run
documentation: Run tasks in this child process continuously until shutdown.
Wraps a child process that executes one task at a time.

documentation: Get a task from the registry.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTaskRegistry._thread_safe_lock
documentation: Context manager for locking across threads (NOT processes). Always swallows exceptions.
Tracks currently-executing tasks in a child process (never shared with the main process).

classes.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.submit_task
documentation: Submit a task to the pending queue.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.pop_task
documentation: Pop a task from the pending queue, increment `_num_tasks_executing`, and add task to registry (if exists).

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.set_started
documentation: Set the event that the child process has started.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.wait_until_started
documentation: Block until the executor sends the "started" event, waiting no longer than `timeout` seconds.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.on_task_complete
documentation: Increment the number of completed tasks and decrement the number currently-executing.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.capacity
documentation: Remaining number of tasks that may be added to this process.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.active
documentation: Number of pending + currently-executing tasks.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.is_shutting_down
documentation: True if the process is shutting down.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.is_idle
documentation: This process is "idle" if no (non-expired) tasks are executing or scheduled.

methods.name: superq.executors.executor_process.ProcessTransceiver.is_idle_ttl_expired
documentation: This process is "idle" if no tasks are executing or scheduled.
2-way data exchange between the main process and a child process using multiprocessing synchronization primitives.

variables.name: superq.tasks.TASK_EPOCH
documentation: Do not change: a fixed reference point for interval tasks

classes.name: superq.tasks.Task

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.is_max_concurrency
documentation: Return True if this task is at or above max concurrency.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.is_max_concurrency_for_kwargs
documentation: Return True if this task is at or above max concurrency.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.on_run_start
documentation:
Set status to RUNNING and check concurrency limits.
Calling function should catch `TaskConcurrencyError`.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.on_run_success
documentation: Set status to SUCCESS and log metrics.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.on_run_failure
documentation: Error handler for tasks that raise an exception during execution.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.run
documentation: Call this task synchronously and update its status in the DB.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.run_aio
documentation: Call this task synchronously and update its status in the DB.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.create
documentation:
Create a new task with the given options and persist it to the DB.
New tasks will be executed by the worker server ASAP (unless `scheduled_for` a later time).

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.reschedule
documentation: Run this task again after its `retry_delay`.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.set_failed
documentation:
Set status=FAILURE and update other failure-related properties of this task.
Returns the list of properties that were modified.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.save
documentation:
Save changes on this instance to the DB. To avoid accidental conflicts, only named fields are updated.
This automatically sets `updated_at` to the current time.

Example:
    task.save(fields=['status'])

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task.fetch
documentation: Return a new copy of this task from the backend.

methods.name: superq.tasks.Task._get_fn
documentation: Get the async function associated with this task.

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.fn_name
documentation: Name of the function being called

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.fn_module
documentation: Module where the function is defined

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.priority
documentation: Lower values are executed first

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.queue_name
documentation: Reserved for future use

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.result_bytes
documentation: Pickled result of the last successful run, or None if task has not succeeded

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.error_type
documentation: E.g. "ERROR" for an exception, TIMEOUT, SIGNAL, etc.

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.num_tries
documentation: Number of *completed* tries, starting from 0

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.num_recovers
documentation: Number of times the task has been restored after becoming stuck or orphaned

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.num_timeouts
documentation: Number of times the task has timed out

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.num_lockouts
documentation: Number of times the task has been rescheduled due to concurrency limits

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.num_ratelimits
documentation: Number of times the task has been rescheduled due to rate limits

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.scheduled_for
documentation: Task won't run until this time (or later)

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.worker_type
documentation: Type of worker that will execute this task

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.worker_host
documentation: Hostname of the worker server that executed this task

class_variables.name: superq.tasks.Task.worker_name
documentation: ID of the worker process or thread that executed this task

Represents a task scheduled to run on a worker server.
This should be lightweight and trivial to serialize to any backend.
